package_description: Carrier-preserving insulation, burner, and heat-recovery retrofit bundle for medium-temperature fossil heat systems.
rollout_limit_notes: Retrofit availability is slower than in low-temperature systems because medium-temperature uses are more heterogeneous and outage windows are harder to generalise at the current role boundary.
evidence_summary: Medium-temperature fossil heat still supports a moderate carrier-preserving retrofit wedge, but it should remain smaller and slower than the low-temperature package.
derivation_method: Apply one proportional multiplier across the direct thermal fuel mix to preserve the authored medium-temperature fossil blend while keeping the package within the accepted research band.
review_notes: Applies only to the fossil incumbent and shares a role-local non-stacking group with the operational controls package.

package_description: Operational controls, tuning, maintenance, and sequencing package on medium-temperature fossil heat systems.
rollout_limit_notes: Operational uptake can move faster than heavy retrofits, but persistence remains management-dependent and the current role is too coarse for a full controls-performance model.
evidence_summary: Australian process-heat guidance supports a smaller operational tuning wedge on medium-temperature fossil systems through controls, sequencing, and maintenance.
derivation_method: Use a conservative proportional multiplier across the direct thermal fuel mix so the package remains clearly smaller than both the route-change methods and the medium-temperature retrofit package.
review_notes: Shares the role-local non-stacking group with the medium-temperature thermal-system retrofit.
